textPlain: Question for the Deutsch HN-ers: Is this readable to your modern eye? Letter for letter I can see the relation to the handwriting I was taught in Dutch in the 80s, but as a text it looks like sanskrit to me. Obviously learnable, like learning greek or other foreign ciphers. But I would not imagine a neighbouring language written down less than a century ago to seem so foreign.
The lowercase e and n look extremely similar!
No. I was taught Sütterlin in elementary school, but I couldn't even begin to read the sample on Wikipedia.
